courses.title: Literature in English (Late 17th through the mid-19th Century)
Literature in English (Mid-19th through the 20th Century)

notes: COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete: 1) The L&S Essential Skills Requirements: Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language; OR 2) IGETC IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (R&C, QR, and FL) or IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of the Essential Skills requirements is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley. For more information on minimum UC eligibility requirements for transfer applicants for fall 2025 and beyond, please visit the UC Berkeley transfer requirements page . Major Requirements: See details on preparation for this major below. Primary selection criteria for admission, in general: - completion of L&S Requirements (or IGETC), plus - strength of academic preparation, and - grade point average. For more information on admission to UC Berkeley: http://admissions.berkeley.edu For more information on majors at UC Berkeley: Berkeley Academic Guide: http://guide.berkeley.edu

To prepare for the English Major, complete UC-transferable courses in literary analysis and the history of literature written in English. These do not include basic composition and technical writing classes. If your course-work has not been approved on Assist for articulation with the Berkeley requirements listed below, retain syllabi and reading lists for evaluation by the department. To complete the English major, students must take no fewer than 12 courses, of which a minimum of 7 need to be upper-division. English 90 and 100 must be taken in sequence prior to English 190. English 45A - Literature in English: Through Milton English 45B - Literature in English: The Late-17th through the Mid-19th Century English 45C - Literature in English: The Mid-19th through the Mid-20th Century A course in Shakespeare (17, 117A, 117B, 117J, or 117S) English 90 - Practices of Literary Study English 100 - The Seminar in Criticism English 190 - The Research Seminar or H195A/B - The Honors Seminar Five English Department elective courses. One of these electives, or else an appropriate 90, 100 or 190, should satisfy the "Literatures in English" breadth requirement. And one upper-division elective, or else an appropriate 100 or 190, should satisfy the "Literature Before 1800" breadth requirement. Notes: The following classes must be taken for a letter grade: English 45A, 45B, 45C, Shakespeare, English 90, 100, and 190. To graduate with a degree in English, a student must achieve at least a 2.0 GPA in: a) all work undertaken at the University of California (all campuses); b) all courses required for the English major, and; c) all upper-division courses in the English major. For more information on the major: Chloe Keller and Jacki Valadez, Undergraduate Advisers Department of English chloekeller@berkeley.edu ; jvaladez@berkeley.edu (510) 642-3467 https://english.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/the_major
